title: Using Numbers with Nouns: Gender and Plural Agreement in Arabic
summary: The session focuses on correctly applying numbers to nouns in Modern Standard Arabic, emphasizing gender agreement and plural forms. The teacher explains how numeric adjectives must agree in gender with the noun they modify and how plurals affect this agreement. Students practice translating sentences using correct grammatical structures.
key_points: Numeric adjectives (e.g., سَبْعَة sabﺏﺎ'a) must agree in gender with the modified noun: female nouns require masculine numbers (e.g., ثلاثىn tis'ى for feminine plurals like حافلات).
Plural nouns take singular numeric adjectives: e.g., ثمانية طبيبات thamaniyyatu tabibat for eight female doctors, not the plural of the number.
Gender agreement extends to dependent words (e.g., sisters إخواتهn iﺏﺡawهt requires masculine numbers like ستة because it's a feminine plural noun).
Harakah rules apply: after kam (how many?), the following word has a fathah (e.g., كام ق迪abatun kama qadibatun).
Masdar and sifah agreement: descriptors must match the noun's grammatical sex, even for plurals of non-intelligent objects.
questions: [00:17:01] Q: Why does 'kam' have a fathah? — A: To maintain harakah consistency in questions like كَم قديabatun.
[00:39:00] Q: Why is 'maftoohatun' used for doors (plural)? — A: Plurals of non-intelligent objects are treated as singular female.

quiz.question: What is the key rule when using numbers (3–10) with nouns in Arabic?
quiz.answer: The number must be opposite in gender to the noun being counted.

quiz.question: If the noun is feminine (e.g., حافلات), what form of the number is used?
quiz.answer: Masculine form of the number is used (e.g., ثلاثُ حافلات / ثماني حافلات depending on context).

quiz.question: Why do we say 'ثمانية طبيبات' instead of a different form?
quiz.answer: Because طبيبات is feminine plural, so the number takes the masculine form and agrees in structure.

quiz.question: What grammatical rule is applied to non-intelligent plural nouns (e.g., doors, classrooms)?
quiz.answer: They are treated as singular feminine for agreement purposes.

quiz.question: What happens to the word after 'كم' (how many) in Arabic questions?
What is the plural of أَخ (brother)?
How do you correctly say 'six brothers' in Arabic using the rules taught?
What is the difference between 'عَمّ' and 'عَمّة'?
quiz.answer: عَمّ is paternal uncle, and عَمّة is paternal aunt.

quran_examples.arabic: لَّيْسَ عَلَى ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ حَرَجٌۭ وَلَا عَلَى ٱلْأَعْرَجِ حَرَجٌۭ وَلَا عَلَى ٱلْمَرِيضِ حَرَجٌۭ وَلَا عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَن تَأْكُلُوا۟ مِنۢ بُيُوتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ ءَابَآئِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ إِخْوَٰنِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَخَوَٰتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَعْمَـٰمِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ عَمَّـٰتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَخْوَٰلِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ خَـٰلَـٰتِكُمْ أَوْ مَا مَلَكْتُم مَّفَاتِحَهُۥٓ أَوْ صَدِيقِكُمْ ۚ لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَأْكُلُوا۟ جَمِيعًا أَوْ أَشْتَاتًۭا ۚ فَإِذَا دَخَلْتُم بُيُوتًۭا فَسَلِّمُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ تَحِيَّةًۭ مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ مُبَـٰرَكَةًۭ طَيِّبَةًۭ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ
quran_examples.transliteration: laysa ʿalā al-aʿmā ḥarajun wa lā ʿalā al-aʿraji ḥarajun wa lā ʿalā al-marīḍi ḥarajun ...
quran_examples.translation: There is no restriction on the blind, nor on the disabled, nor on the sick... nor on yourselves if you eat from your homes... This is how Allah makes His signs clear to you so that you may understand.
quran_examples.note: Illustrates plural family nouns in iḍāfa structure (e.g., إِخْوَٰنِكُمْ, أَخَوَٰتِكُمْ, عَمَّٰتِكُمْ), reinforcing plural agreement and noun relationships in Arabic grammar.
